data.body:
Goal:
Introduce canonical agent identity fields in Knots-related Foolery config so lease metadata can be produced without guessing from display labels or overloading the CLI model identifier. Keep the existing `model` field as the raw CLI model/config value, add explicit lease identity fields for the data Knots needs, add provider-specific parsing for Codex, Claude, and OpenCode, rewrite or backfill legacy configs into the canonical shape, and make pool references resolve from persisted agent ids instead of runtime naming conventions.

Verification:
1. Run targeted settings coverage for schema/load/scan/migration behavior and confirm Codex, Claude, and OpenCode examples load and save with stable canonical identity fields while preserving the raw `model` value.
2. Inspect representative written TOML fixtures or `~/.config/foolery/settings.toml` output and confirm the canonical lease identity fields are present, legacy label-derived data is removed or ignored, and pool agent ids resolve from persisted config data.
3. Validate legacy-config rewrite/backfill on representative old settings fixtures and confirm the rewritten config preserves routing while normalizing lease identity data.

Constraints:
- Do not repurpose the existing `model` field; it must remain the raw identifier/config value used by the agent CLI.
- Remove display `label` as an authoritative identity source; display strings should be derived from canonical fields.
- Cover Codex, Claude, and OpenCode explicitly; do not rely on one generic parser for all three.
data.title: Canonicalize agent lease identity config
